_schema.description: Workouts pending push to Intervals.icu. Claude writes here; push.py reads and clears.

step_types.ramp: Linear ramp from power to power_end
step_types.repeat: Repeat block — requires reps + nested steps array

_schema.workout_doc_note: Use 'steps' (simplified) OR 'workout_doc' (raw Intervals.icu format). Not both. Omit both for description-only workouts.
_schema.ftp_note: Top-level 'ftp' field used for power resolution when --ftp flag not set on CLI

workouts._comment: EXAMPLE 1 — Simple description-only workout (no structured steps)
workouts.name: Aktive Erholung — Laufen
workouts.description:
- 30m Z1 Z1-Z2 HR Z1 Pace

Lockeres Auslaufen nach dem Rennen. HR unter 145 bpm halten.

workouts._comment: EXAMPLE 2 — Structured workout using simplified 'steps' format (push.py builds workout_doc)
workouts.name: Erholung — Rad Z1
workouts.description: Lockeres Spinning. Power unter 122 W, HR unter 130 bpm.

workouts._comment: EXAMPLE 3 — Sweetspot intervals with warm-up and cool-down (simplified steps)
workouts.name: Grundlage — Rad Z2
workouts.description: Erster längerer Ride. Power 102–152 W, HR unter 162 bpm.

workouts._comment: EXAMPLE 4 — Interval session with repeat block (simplified steps)
workouts.name: 5x5min Sweetspot
workouts.description: 5x5min @ 88-93% FTP / 2.5min recovery. Fokus auf gleichmäßige Power.

workouts._comment: EXAMPLE 5 — Raw workout_doc (pre-built Intervals.icu format, skip push.py builder)
workouts.name: Bouldern — Technik
workouts.description: Bouldern — Fokus auf Technik, keine Kraftmaximalversuche.
